file_name: GentechHoldingsInc_20190808_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11776814_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Distributor Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client come to be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, must be approved by the company prior to any sale. After official approval from the Company, the customer introduced by Distributor, will appear on an official list in this contract, and will only be met by the Distributor and will follow the rules of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client come to be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, may or may not require approval by the company prior to any sale, depending on the company's current sales strategies. After official approval from the Company, should approval be deemed necessary, the customer introduced by Distributor, will appear on an official list in this contract, and will only be met by the Distributor and will follow the rules of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'must be approved by the company prior to any sale' to 'may or may not require approval by the company prior to any sale, depending on the company's current sales strategies' this change introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original intent. This creates a contradiction about the exclusivity commitment and also creates uncertainty on the distributor on when they need to take approval from the company, and when they dont need to take approval.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company desires to appoint Distributor as its exclusive distributor of the Products to all Clients that Distributor introduce, and upon confirmed the first order, this client come to be exclusive of Distributor, every new customer introduced by Distributor, may or may not require approval by the company prior to any sale, depending on the company's current sales strategies. After official approval from the Company, should approval be deemed necessary, the customer introduced by Distributor, will appear on an official list in this contract, and will only be met by the Distributor and will follow the rules of this agreement. Distributor desires such appointment subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement, including any exhibits or schedules attached hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contingency ('may or may not require approval...depending on the company's current sales strategies') where the original text implied a requirement for approval ('must be approved by the company prior to any sale'). This creates a direct contradiction regarding the approval process for new customers, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. This uncertainty about approval directly undermines the exclusivity granted to the distributor, which is a key term within the agreement and listed in both sections of text, which constitutes as a structural flaw in the contract.

perturbation.original_text: The rights granted Distributor hereunder are granted for the following geographical areas and markets. USA.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights granted Distributor hereunder are granted for the following geographical areas and markets. USA, and potentially other territories at the Company's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the defined Territory from 'USA' to 'USA, and potentially other territories at the Company's sole discretion' introduces a direct contradiction. The original text specifies USA only, while the revised text opens the possibility of other territories, but only at the Company's discretion. This affects the scope of the agreement and the exclusivity granted to the distributor. It makes it unclear whether the distributor has exclusive rights within those additional territories.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Distributor Agreement, Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell and distribute the Products to customers that Distributor introduce and confirmed by Company, (in all territory of United State of America (USA) and to render other services as a distributor for Company as set forth herein. Distributor shall limit its activities with respect to the Products to Customers located within the Territory and refrain from selling or otherwise transferring, directly or indirectly, the Products to any person outside the Territory, without the express written consent of Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the territory to the "USA", granting the distributor exclusive rights within that territory as stated in the contradicted text. The changed text adds "and potentially other territories at the Company's sole discretion," creating a contradiction. This addition undermines the distributor's exclusive rights in the USA, as the company can unilaterally expand the territory, diluting the distributor's exclusivity and contradicting the original grant of exclusive rights limited to the USA in the contradicted text. The 'sole discretion' aspect further exacerbates the contradiction, allowing the Company to add territories without the Distributor's consent, impacting the scope of the Distributor's exclusive rights as defined in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: Distributor will promptly inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. Within Seven (7) days of receipt of such Products, Distributor will notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, and Company will promptly replace such Products free of charge.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributor will promptly inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. While Distributor is expected to notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, Company's obligation to replace such Products free of charge is subject to internal review and approval.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'While Distributor is expected to notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, Company's obligation to replace such Products free of charge is subject to internal review and approval.' The new sentence contradicts the guarantee of replacement, introducing uncertainty. This weakens the distributor's rights as it relies on the company's subjective review.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor will promptly inspect the Products upon receipt at Distributor's facility to determine whether any Products included in the shipment are in short supply, defective, or otherwise not in conformance with this Agreement. While Distributor is expected to notify Company of any shortages, defects, non-conformance, Company's obligation to replace such Products free of charge is subject to internal review and approval. Title to the Products shall pass to Distributor upon delivery.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the company's obligation to replace products free of charge is subject to internal review and approval. This directly conflicts with the implied guarantee of replacement in the original text and the expected obligation in the contradicted text, creating uncertainty and weakening the distributor's rights. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the addition of the contingency regarding internal review directly contradicts the original obligation, causing uncertainty about the rights of each party.
